title: Frosted Smoky Egyptian Tofu Noodles
description: A smoky, crispy, energizing Ethiopian-inspired dish featuring flat rice noodles, tofu, and an ice-bath shock, served from a bamboo steamer.
instructions: Drain tofu and press to remove excess moisture.
Toss tofu cubes with smoked paprika, soy sauce, maple syrup, sesame oil, garlic powder, and cumin until fully coated.
Spread the coated tofu on a bamboo steamer lined with parchment, place into a pot of boiling water, and steam for 10 minutes until firm.
While tofu steams, soak flat rice noodles in hot water for 3 minutes, then drain and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk lemon juice with a splash of water to create a light citrus glaze.
Remove the steamer and let the tofu rest for one minute, then dip each cube in the citrus glaze for a crisp finish.
Assemble the dish by arranging noodles in the steamer basket, topping with glazed tofu, and sprinkling sesame seeds and cilantro.
Shock the assembled noodles in an ice bath of 200ml ice water for 30 seconds to stop cooking and enhance crispness.
Drain the noodles and immediately transfer to a serving plate, drizzling any remaining glaze from the tofu over them.
Serve hot, optionally adding extra lemon wedges for a refreshing citrus kick.
